raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Making the section stronger centrally involves tightening the contribution claim around JudgeMT and the self-preference-bias framing.","R1.2":"The delegation calls for checking whether the argument from model-family separation to bias elimination and practical substitution is logically defensible.","R1.3":"The artifact makes empirical claims about correlations, score patterns, and bias reduction, so strengthening requires aligning those claims with the reported evidence.","R1.6":"The broad phrasing also includes sentence- and paragraph-level improvements to clarity, force, and academic register.","RX.1":"Uncertainty must be disclosed because the evidence reported may not fully establish bias elimination or broad generalization.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing because the artifact currently moves from limited comparisons to strong claims about eliminating bias and replacing human evaluation.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is required to keep the strengthening within the paper-writing task rather than executing new experiments or external audits.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is relevant because overstating automated evaluation as a human-evaluation substitute could mislead practitioners.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability are needed so revisions preserve clear links to the reported WMT22 setup, metrics, and observed results."}}

rationale.R1.1: Making the section stronger centrally involves tightening the contribution claim around JudgeMT and the self-preference-bias framing.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation calls for checking whether the argument from model-family separation to bias elimination and practical substitution is logically defensible.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical claims about correlations, score patterns, and bias reduction, so strengthening requires aligning those claims with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.6: The broad phrasing also includes sentence- and paragraph-level improvements to clarity, force, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty must be disclosed because the evidence reported may not fully establish bias elimination or broad generalization.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing because the artifact currently moves from limited comparisons to strong claims about eliminating bias and replacing human evaluation.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is required to keep the strengthening within the paper-writing task rather than executing new experiments or external audits.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is relevant because overstating automated evaluation as a human-evaluation substitute could mislead practitioners.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are needed so revisions preserve clear links to the reported WMT22 setup, metrics, and observed results.
